parts.title: The Assignment
parts.text: Agent Zara received the message at exactly midnight. Her **aim** was clear — find the stolen documents before dawn. She took a moment to **review** her notes one final time, checking every detail for even the smallest **error**. There was no room for mistakes on a mission this important.

parts.title: Into Danger
parts.text: Zara crept along the rocky **coast**, where the crashing waves masked the sound of her footsteps. She was careful not to **mention** her location on the radio — the enemy might be listening. A hidden **passage** ran beneath the old warehouse, and she squeezed through it without pausing to **halt**. The wet walls began to **glisten** in the dim beam of her torch.

parts.title: The Vault
parts.text: Inside, she found the safe — half-buried in a muddy **marsh** of old papers and broken shelves. It held the stolen **provisions** maps that the spy network needed to **release** to the public. Arnav had told her that the library archives were the most valuable **resource** for tracing the originals. "I've found them," she whispered into her earpiece to Saanvi.

parts.title: Mission Complete
parts.text: Agent Zara escaped just as the sun rose. The documents revealed a network of stolen art worth millions. Arnav met her at the safe house with a rare smile. "Outstanding work," he said. "You've just solved a case that's been open for fifteen years." Zara allowed herself a small smile. Another mission complete — but she knew the next one was already waiting.

definitions.meaning1: To point or direct toward a target. Example: She aimed the ball at the basket.
definitions.meaning2: A goal or purpose. Example: His aim is to become a doctor.
definitions.studentChallenge: Her ___ was to finish the race.

definitions.meaning1: To stop moving or doing something. Example: The crossing guard raised her hand to halt the traffic so the children could cross safely.
definitions.meaning2: A complete stop or pause. Example: The train came to a halt at the station.
definitions.studentChallenge: The teacher asked everyone to ___ what they were doing and listen to the announcement.

definitions.meaning1: To set free or let go. Example: The wildlife centre decided to release the healed owl back into the forest.
definitions.meaning2: To make something available to the public. Example: The studio will release the new film next Friday.
definitions.studentChallenge: The children cheered as the volunteers began to ___ the baby sea turtles into the ocean.

definitions.meaning1: A mistake or something done incorrectly. Example: She found an error in her math homework and fixed it before turning it in.
definitions.studentChallenge: The spelling ___ in his report was easy to fix once his teacher pointed it out.

definitions.meaning1: A careful look at something to check it or give an opinion about it. Example: The film review in the newspaper said the movie was exciting and fun for the whole family.
definitions.meaning2: To look at something again to study or check it. Example: We need to review our notes before the quiz tomorrow.
definitions.studentChallenge: The teacher wrote a glowing ___ of the student's essay, praising her creativity and grammar.

definitions.meaning1: The land next to the sea. Example: We drove along the coast to see the ocean.
definitions.meaning2: To move without effort. Example: The bike coasted down the hill.
definitions.studentChallenge: Beautiful beaches line the ___ of California.

definitions.meaning1: To briefly talk about or refer to something. Example: Did anyone mention what time the soccer game starts tomorrow?
definitions.studentChallenge: She forgot to ___ that the meeting had been moved to a different room.

definitions.meaning1: A section of a book or piece of writing. Example: Read the passage and answer the questions.
definitions.meaning2: A narrow path or corridor. Example: The passage led to a secret room.
definitions.studentChallenge: The ___ in the book described a beautiful garden.

definitions.meaning1: An area of low, wet land often covered with grasses and water. Example: Frogs and herons make their homes in the muddy marsh near the river.
definitions.studentChallenge: We wore tall rubber boots to walk through the ___ without getting our feet soaked.

definitions.meaning1: Supplies of food and other things needed for a journey or period of time. Example: The explorers packed enough provisions for a two-week trek through the jungle.
definitions.studentChallenge: Before setting sail, the crew loaded the ship with ___ including bread, dried fruit, and water.

definitions.meaning1: Something useful that can be used when needed. Example: Water is an important natural resource.
definitions.studentChallenge: The library is a great ___ for research.

definitions.meaning1: To sparkle or shine with reflected light. Example: The morning dew glistened on the grass.
definitions.studentChallenge: The snow ___ in the bright sunlight.

doubleTakeQuiz.sentence1: She carefully ___ the arrow at the target on the far wall and released it perfectly.
doubleTakeQuiz.sentence2: Her ___ for the summer was to learn to swim fifty metres without stopping.

doubleTakeQuiz.sentence1: The crossing guard raised her hand to ___ the traffic so the children could cross safely.
doubleTakeQuiz.sentence2: The teacher asked everyone to ___ what they were doing and listen to the important announcement.

doubleTakeQuiz.sentence1: The wildlife centre decided to ___ the healed owl back into the forest, setting it free once more.
doubleTakeQuiz.sentence2: The crowd cheered as the volunteers began to ___ the rescued sea turtles into the ocean.

doubleTakeQuiz.sentence1: She found an ___ in her math homework — a wrong number she had written by mistake — and fixed it.
doubleTakeQuiz.sentence2: The spelling ___ in his report was easy to correct once his teacher pointed it out.

doubleTakeQuiz.sentence1: The film ___ in the newspaper called it the most exciting adventure movie of the year.
doubleTakeQuiz.sentence2: The teacher wrote a glowing ___ of the student's essay, praising her creativity and grammar.

doubleTakeQuiz.sentence1: We drove along the rocky ___ for hours, watching the waves crash against the cliffs below.
doubleTakeQuiz.sentence2: Beautiful sandy beaches line the ___ of California, stretching for hundreds of kilometres.

doubleTakeQuiz.sentence1: Did anyone ___ what time the football match starts? I only heard it briefly in passing.
doubleTakeQuiz.sentence2: She forgot to ___ that the meeting had been moved to a different room.

doubleTakeQuiz.sentence1: Read the short ___ carefully, then answer the five questions at the bottom of the page.
doubleTakeQuiz.sentence2: The ___ in the book described a beautiful moonlit garden in vivid detail.

doubleTakeQuiz.sentence1: Frogs and herons make their homes in the muddy, waterlogged ___ near the river.
doubleTakeQuiz.sentence2: We wore tall rubber boots to wade through the soggy ___ without getting our feet soaked.

doubleTakeQuiz.sentence1: The explorers packed enough ___ for a two-week trek — food, water, and medical supplies.
doubleTakeQuiz.sentence2: Before setting sail, the crew loaded the ship with ___ including dried meat, biscuits, and fresh water.

doubleTakeQuiz.sentence1: Clean water is the most valuable natural ___ on Earth — nothing can survive without it.
doubleTakeQuiz.sentence2: The library is an excellent ___ for research, with thousands of books and databases to explore.

doubleTakeQuiz.sentence1: The morning dew made every blade of grass ___ like tiny diamonds in the early light.
doubleTakeQuiz.sentence2: The freshly fallen snow began to ___ brilliantly as the sun broke through the clouds.

secretPassage.instructions: Read the passage below. Fill in each blank with the correct vocabulary word from the word bank.
secretPassage.passage: Agent Dara's _(1)_ was clear: find the stolen map before midnight, focusing directly on the old lighthouse as her target. She did not slow down or _(2)_ for a single moment — stopping now would cost her the mission. The park ranger had agreed to _(3)_ the trained falcon once the thief was caught, setting it free to fly back into the wild. Every _(4)_ the thief made left behind a clue — a small mistake that Dara carefully photographed. She stopped to _(5)_ her notes, reading them over once more to check for anything she had missed. The narrow cliff path hugged the _(6)_, running right alongside the crashing sea below. She radioed her partner to _(7)_ the extra guards — just a quick note, nothing more. A hidden _(8)_ beneath the floorboards led deep under the building, its narrow corridor barely wide enough to squeeze through. Near the entrance of the tunnel, muddy water pooled in a low, soggy _(9)_ thick with reeds and frogs. Dara had packed her _(10)_ carefully: water, a torch, rope, and energy bars — everything needed for the night ahead. Clean water was her most precious _(11)_, so she saved every drop. The wet stone walls began to _(12)_ in the torchlight, each droplet sparkling like a tiny silver jewel.

wordScaleImposter.studentPick: Pick 2 imposters and explain why they are opposites of the vocabulary word.
